title: First Installable, Setup, and Testable Ehecoatl Tasklist
mainGoal: Reach the first installable, setup, and testable version of Ehecoatl, with a system that already works in a safe start-to-end manner before performance tasks and optimizations begin.

tasklistPolicy.orderingRule: Ordered by delivery priority, where earlier groups remove blockers for later groups.
tasklistPolicy.focusRule: Correctness, bootability, setup usability, and safe lifecycle behavior come before performance work.
tasklistPolicy.readerNote: This tasklist is derived from the current system reports under report/system. Read the referenced reports for the full reasoning behind each task group.

sourceReports.topic: Process supervision, child lifecycle, restart, shutdown
HTTP request execution pipeline and response flow
Tenancy routing, tenant controller flow, setup-time tenant template
Plugin hook model, discovery/loading, bundled plugin readiness

taskGroups.title: Unblock Core Boot And Plugin Loading
taskGroups.goal: Make the runtime boot predictably with its configured plugin system and without immediate startup-blocking loader failures.
pointsOfAttention: This is the first blocker because every process context scans plugins during bootstrap.
The mandatory bundled plugin path must resolve before MAIN, ENGINE, MANAGER, or TENANT can load consistently.
Do not leave config/plugin inventory mismatches unresolved, or later debugging will be noisy and misleading.

tasks.title: Repair mandatory bundled plugin resolution
tasks.reportedGoal: Replace or satisfy the current @plugins dependency so plugin-scan can resolve the bundled plugin root reliably.

subtasks.title: Choose one bundled plugin root strategy and apply it consistently
options: replace require.resolve('@plugins') with a direct repository-local plugins path strategy

subtasks.title: Verify plugin discovery works in MAIN, ENGINE, MANAGER, and TENANT bootstrap flows
Add a simple boot verification path that confirms plugin scanning no longer fails at process start

tasks.title: Reconcile plugin inventory and plugin identity
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure configured plugins, filenames, and exported names describe the same plugin set.

subtasks.title: Remove or disable plugin entries from default config that are not actually present
Normalize plugin identity across config key, filename, and exported plugin name
Decide whether placeholder plugins should be implemented, disabled, or removed from the default inventory

taskGroups.title: Repair Lazy Loading And Frozen Gateway Contracts
taskGroups.goal: Make gateway and shared-service adapter loading compatible with the current object lifecycle so runtime entry paths can execute without structural failures.
pointsOfAttention: This is a cross-cutting blocker affecting request pipeline and shared services.
The chosen fix should be applied consistently, not as isolated one-off patches.
Prefer one clear lifecycle rule: load adapters before freezing, or stop mutating frozen instances at lazy-load time.

tasks.title: Define the adapter-loading lifecycle rule
tasks.reportedGoal: Choose and implement one consistent pattern for adapter loading across GatewayCore users.

subtasks.title: Audit gateways that freeze themselves before calling GatewayCore.loadAdapter()
Refactor RequestPipeline and shared services to comply with the chosen rule
Smoke-test adapter loading in request pipeline, storage service, and shared cache service

taskGroups.title: Make Tenancy Routing Actually Work
taskGroups.goal: Produce a reliable manager-side tenant scan and route-match path that can supply valid tenant routes to the engine.
pointsOfAttention: Do not patch only the symptoms in HTTP; tenancy must first return valid route data consistently.
Keep scan readiness, cache behavior, and dynamic route replacement in scope together.
The first successful smoke test depends on this group being complete.

tasks.title: Fix tenancy scanning and host metadata assembly
tasks.reportedGoal: Make the default tenancy adapter compatible with the bundled storage adapter and directory-entry shape.

subtasks.title: Repair the scanTenantsAdapter path-building contract so it does not depend on missing parentPath metadata
Verify domain config and host config merging produces stable host route objects
Ensure rescan logic handles stale or deleted hosts predictably

tasks.title: Remove hard runtime dependency on global route replacer state
tasks.reportedGoal: Make dynamic route replacement self-contained and deterministic.

subtasks.title: Replace global.responser.Replacer usage with a local, explicit replacement utility
Cover static and dynamic route matching with small direct tests or smoke scripts

tasks.title: Guarantee first-scan readiness before route-serving traffic
tasks.reportedGoal: Avoid serving requests before the initial tenant map has been built.

subtasks.title: Choose whether manager startup should await the first scan or expose a temporary not-ready state
Add a visible readiness check so early route misses are not silent startup races

taskGroups.title: Repair The HTTP Request And Response Path
taskGroups.goal: Make the HTTP execution path work start-to-finish for a basic request, including route resolution, controller dispatch, and response writing.
pointsOfAttention: This group should end with one working controller route and one working asset route.
Fix order matters: route resolution must happen before body-read and pipeline logic that depends on tenantRoute.
Keep request mutability rules explicit to avoid new contract mismatches.

tasks.title: Resolve tenant route before body/session/pipeline dependencies
tasks.reportedGoal: Make executionContext.tenantRoute available before any code path that depends on it.

subtasks.title: Insert route resolution into the live HTTP flow before CSRF, body handling, redirect checks, and stage execution
Verify redirect and asset/controller branches see a valid TenantRoute

tasks.title: Fix RequestData and response-envelope mutation contracts
tasks.reportedGoal: Allow legitimate request and response mutation paths without violating frozen object contracts.

subtasks.title: Make requestData.body writable during parsing or move body data into a writable layer
Make response cookie writes safe from the first assignment path onward

tasks.title: Repair bundled pipeline stages for first working HTTP flow
tasks.reportedGoal: Make the default stage sequence executable and useful for the first safe version.

subtasks.title: Repair local-file-stream-stage argument flow, async storage usage, and stream response integration
Decide whether mid-rate-limit-stage should be implemented now or removed from the default sequence temporarily
Ensure tenant-controller-stage maps successful and failed controller responses into appropriate HTTP responses

tasks.title: Align helper utilities with the active uWS transport
tasks.reportedGoal: Remove Node-http response API assumptions from the active uWS path.

subtasks.title: Adapt or replace request-limiter-http to use uWS-compatible response semantics
Verify error, abort, and write hooks still behave as intended after the transport alignment

taskGroups.title: Create The First Real Tenant Smoke-Test Scaffold
taskGroups.goal: Make setup and tenant onboarding produce a fast, predictable smoke-test path with both controller and asset coverage.
pointsOfAttention: The first installable version should not require manual multi-file tenant authoring just to prove the system works.
This group should serve human usability and automated smoke testing at the same time.
Keep the scaffold minimal and intentionally disposable.

tasks.title: Define a minimal first-tenant template
tasks.reportedGoal: Provide one simple tenant with a controller route and a static asset route for immediate validation.

subtasks.title: Create a minimal domain config and host config shape with routesAvailable
Add one sample controller and one sample public asset
Define the expected smoke requests and expected responses

tasks.title: Integrate the template into setup or tenant onboarding
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure a fresh install can reach a first working tenant without manual low-level assembly.

subtasks.title: Choose whether the scaffold is created by setup.sh, tenant_create, or a dedicated scaffold command
Apply tenant ACL and access preparation as part of the onboarding flow, not only proxy_setup
Update the getting-started flow so the scaffolded path is the default smoke-test story

taskGroups.title: Harden Process Supervision For Safe Start-To-End Operation
taskGroups.goal: Make process launch, exit, crash, restart, and main shutdown behavior reliable enough for a first safe operational version.
pointsOfAttention: This group is about safety and predictability, not just booting once.
Unexpected child exits need a clear policy, not only heartbeat-based recovery.
Shutdown reporting should describe reality, not just promise fulfillment.

tasks.title: Complete unexpected-exit supervision and restart policy
tasks.reportedGoal: Classify and recover from child crashes outside the heartbeat-only path.

subtasks.title: Define how unexpected child exit is distinguished from orderly shutdown
Implement restart behavior for non-terminal unexpected exits where appropriate
Verify DEAD remains terminal and non-restarting

tasks.title: Repair post-spawn failure and reload resource hygiene
tasks.reportedGoal: Avoid leaked or stale state around partially started or restarted children.

subtasks.title: Kill and unregister children if initAdapter or LAUNCH.AFTER fails after spawn
Stop reusing stale resources and cleanupTasks across reloads

tasks.title: Strengthen main-process coordinated shutdown
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure main can always cleanly bring down supervised children and report truthfully.

subtasks.title: Register main child-shutdown protection before the first child can be spawned
Make shutdownProcess and shutdownAllChildren reflect real teardown completion and success
Improve fatal error logging to preserve original error objects

taskGroups.title: Produce The First Installable And Testable Release Candidate
taskGroups.goal: Turn the repaired runtime into a reproducible install-and-smoke-test flow that proves Ehecoatl works from setup to served traffic.
pointsOfAttention: This group should close the gap between code correctness and operator confidence.
The result should be executable by someone following docs on a fresh machine.
Keep smoke tests narrow and concrete.

tasks.title: Define first-release smoke criteria
tasks.reportedGoal: Create a small, decisive list of checks that prove the first safe version works.

subtasks.title: Boot main successfully with plugin scanning enabled
Complete one tenant route resolution and one tenant process spawn
Serve one controller response and one static asset response
Stop the system cleanly and confirm supervised children exit safely

tasks.title: Align setup, CLI, docs, and smoke flow
tasks.reportedGoal: Make installation and first-run instructions match the real working path.

subtasks.title: Update setup and tenant onboarding docs to use the working smoke-test path
Document the first expected routes and outputs for the scaffold tenant
Add a local validation script or documented command sequence for release checks

taskGroups.title: Run Performance Baselines And Targeted Improvements
taskGroups.goal: Begin performance work only after the system is installable, safe, and end-to-end testable.
pointsOfAttention: Do not optimize around broken or placeholder flows.
Start with measurement, not guesswork.
Use the smoke-test tenant as the first controlled benchmark target.

tasks.title: Create baseline measurements for the first safe version
tasks.reportedGoal: Measure boot, request latency, throughput, and restart/shutdown timings on a stable system.

subtasks.title: Measure cold start and first-scan readiness time
Measure controller route latency and static asset latency
Measure child restart and shutdown timings under supervision

tasks.title: Prioritize post-baseline optimizations
tasks.reportedGoal: Choose improvements based on measured bottlenecks rather than assumptions.

subtasks.title: Review route cache behavior, queue behavior, and controller/asset hot paths
Review heartbeat and supervision overhead only after correctness is proven
Track whether plugin hooks add meaningful overhead to the request path

releaseGateForFirstUsableVersion.description: Performance work begins only after Ehecoatl is installable, setup-ready, plugin-bootable, tenant-routable, HTTP-functional, and safely stoppable.
